title: Jedi Apprentice: The Deadly Hunter
content:
{{Top|rwm|imp|italics=1}} {{Redlink}} {{Book |image=[[File:JA11Deadly_Hunter.jpg]] |title=''Jedi Apprentice: The Deadly Hunter'' |author=[[Jude Watson]]''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' |cover artist=[[Cliff Nielsen]] |illustrator= |editor= |publisher=[[Scholastic]] |early release= |release date=[[December]] [[2000]]{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/young/news20000829.html|text=Upcoming ''Jedi Apprentices''}} |intl release= |rereleased= |media type=Paperback |pages= |isbn=0-439-13930-9 |isbn2= |isbn3= |timeline=[[43 BBY/Legends|43 BBY]] |series=''[[Star Wars: Jedi Apprentice]]'' |preceded by=[[Jedi Apprentice: The Shattered Peace|''The Shattered Peace'']] |followed by=[[Jedi Apprentice: The Evil Experiment|''The Evil Experiment'']] }} '''''Jedi Apprentice: The Deadly Hunter''''' by [[Jude Watson]] is the eleventh in a series of young reader novels called '''''[[Star Wars: Jedi Apprentice]]'''''. All the books in the series are written by [[Jude Watson]], except for the first book, [[Jedi Apprentice: The Rising Force|The Rising Force]], which was written by [[Dave Wolverton]]. The series explores the adventures of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] prior to [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. ==Publisher's summary==
'''Peace over Anger
Honor over Hate
Strength over Fear'''
''Nobody knows her name. Nobody knows when she will strike. All they know is that she is a deadly bounty hunter—and her latest mission has taken her to Coruscant, home of the Jedi''. ''Her target: an old friend of Qui-Gon Jinn's.'' ''Qui-Gon and his apprentice, Obi-Wan Kenobi, make an attempt to catch her...and fail. Now they are her targets, too.'' ==Plot summary== [[Didi Oddo]], an [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]] and longtime friend of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], runs a [[Cafe/Legends|café]] in [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant’s]] [[Senate District/Legends|Senate District]]. He finds himself the target of a mysterious [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]]. Qui-Gon and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan]] are on the case. Together they uncover a [[Death/Legends|murder]] mystery that implicates renown [[Scientist/Legends|scientist]] [[Jenna Zan Arbor]]. As the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] close in on the answer, Qui-Gon is captured by the hunter. ==Continuity== Another break in time occurred between this and the [[Jedi Apprentice: The Shattered Peace|previous book]], advancing the timeline by [[Standard month/Legends|months]] and aging [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] to fourteen. This marked the first appearance of [[Didi Oddo]], who in the future sold his [[Cafe/Legends|café]] to [[Dexter Jettster/Legends|Dexter Jettster]]. ==Media== ===Cover gallery=== This is a gallery of the different cover variations of ''Jedi Apprentice: The Deadly Hunter''.
